commit.message:
Fix double-slash in release-lock URL causing 404

The acquire-lock URL correctly used ${baseUrl}api/... but all three
release-lock URLs had ${baseUrl}/api/... which produced //api/... since
baseUrl already ends with /. The hosting emulator doesn't normalize
double slashes, so the path didn't match the rewrite rule.

eliminating the blank white/dark flash on every page load.

Two bugs found during E2E re-test:

1. DeviceStatusContext.initializeDeviceStatuses() didn't notify existing
   subscribers — indicators mounted before API init completed stayed
   at default "offline" even when API returned "online"

2. Devices page referenced device.lastSeen but API returns
   device.lastHeartbeat — always showed "Never"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix magic link consumed by email security scanners

Email scanners (Gmail, Outlook SafeLinks, etc.) follow GET links
automatically, consuming the one-time token before the user clicks.

Fix: GET /auth/verify now only validates the token and shows a
confirmation page. POST /auth/verify actually consumes it.
Scanners never POST forms, so the token is preserved for the user.

commit.message:
docs(architecture): document SQL RPC enterprise-grade scalability solution

Replaces fetchAllLedgerEntries() temporary fix with permanent SQL RPC
approach. Explains why RPCs are state-of-the-art:
- No 1000-row truncation limit
- O(1) performance (instant, not pagination)
- Scales to 1M tenants × 1M transactions/each
- Enterprise-grade, production-ready

commit.message:
[feature] Peer review pipeline: 4-model deliberation, surgical revision, improvement verification

4 AI reviewers (Gemini 3.1 Pro, Gemini 3 Flash, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.6)
simulate top-journal peer review with 3-round structured deliberation (independent
reviews, rebuttals, consensus). Opus 4.6 revision agent makes surgical edits, then
a second review cycle verifies improvement. Scores 15 attributes (1-100), stores
results in session DB and central DB, outputs to output_final/ with full review
artifacts. Runs by default after main pipeline, disable with --no-peer-review.

commit.message:
Harden app for multi-user concurrency (5 warehouse users)

1. Sync lock — prevents double-tap or accidental concurrent sync on
   the same device. Shows "Sync already in progress" if tapped again.

2. Graph API retry with backoff — automatic retry (up to 3 attempts)
   for HTTP 409 (Conflict), 429 (Rate Limit), and 503 (Server Busy).
   Uses Retry-After header when present, otherwise exponential backoff
   (1s, 2s, 4s). Covers both graph() and graphRaw() functions.

3. Non-throwing sync loop — previously the first failed record threw
   an error and stopped all remaining records from syncing. Now marks
   failed records as FAILED and continues with the rest. Reports
   "X synced, Y failed" at the end so users know what to retry.

4. KPI refresh guard — prevents concurrent KPI sheet rebuilds when
   two users sync at the same time. Second caller skips gracefully.

docs: comprehensive data model analysis — entities, access patterns, storage strategy

Thorough research covering current model inventory (13 storage backends),
access pattern analysis for all entities, pain points (fragile task IDs,
split-brain storage, write-only PostgreSQL), and evaluation of six data
model approaches (event sourcing, CQRS, actor model, document store,
graph model, time-series). Proposes hybrid architecture with SQLite event
store, structured task storage, and unified event schema. Includes
language-specific analysis mapping data model requirements to Python,
Go, Rust, TypeScript, and Elixir capabilities.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add trimLeft/trimRight aliases to StringPrototype

JavaScript's trimLeft/trimRight are legacy aliases for trimStart/trimEnd.
Libraries like cliui use trimRight/trimLeft, causing MethodCall to return
undefined (rendered as literal "undefined" text in yargs help output).

commit.message:
Fix tabular encoder inference gap, sanitize model_id on all endpoints, clean up frontend

Critical: Inference engine now loads and applies the trained tabular
encoder (GRN/MLP) from tabular_encoder_state.pth during prediction.
Previously, the encoder was saved by Phase 7 but never loaded — raw
preprocessed features were fed directly to the fusion head, causing
dimension mismatch and incorrect predictions for models trained with
learned tabular encoders.

Security: Added _sanitize_model_id() to /predict (sync) and /ws/predict
(WebSocket) endpoints. These were the only two prediction paths missing
directory traversal validation.

Frontend: Removed non-functional image ZIP uploader from batch prediction
mode (was displayed but never processed). Added guidance for image paths
in CSV files instead.

Doc: Fixed /predict docstring claiming target_class defaults to 0 when
the code defaults to -1 (auto-targeting predicted class).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: rewrite autoconfig env apply — avoid fragile eval+f-string

Replace eval with while-read loop parsing KEY=VALUE lines from Python.
Uses ${!var:-} indirect expansion to check if var is already set.
Fixes "bad substitution" error on AmpereOne.

commit.message:
Add GitHub Pages deployment with SPA routing support

- app.config.js: reads EXPO_PUBLIC_BASE_URL env var so asset paths are
  prefixed with /bb-study-app/ in CI and empty locally
- .github/workflows/deploy.yml: builds with expo export then deploys
  dist/ to GitHub Pages via actions/deploy-pages@v4
- public/404.html: catches unknown paths on GitHub Pages, stores the
  intended route in sessionStorage, redirects to app root
- public/index.html: custom Expo HTML template that restores the saved
  path via history.replaceState so Expo Router picks up the right route
- public/manifest.json: switched icon src and start_url to relative
  paths (./icon-192.png, ./) so PWA works at any base path

Enhance Generator Oracle with expanded ECM diagnostics and subsystem coverage

- Add 6 new ECM manufacturers to ECMDiagnosticsPanel:
  - Detroit Diesel DDEC VI
  - John Deere PowerTech
  - Deutz EMR4
  - Yanmar ECU
  - Doosan/Daewoo ECM
  - Weichai/Baudouin ECM

- Expand controllerFaultCodes.ts with new categories:
  - ECM/Engine Control (7000-7999): Communication, J1939, injectors, rail pressure, EGR, SCR, DPF, VGT, timing
  - Cooling System (8000-8199): Flow, thermostat, pump, fan, heat exchanger
  - Lubrication System (8200-8399): Oil flow, filter, cooler, quality
  - Air System (8400-8499): Filter, intake, charge air
  - Exhaust (8500-8599): Temperature, back pressure
  - Wiring (8600-8799): Harness, connectors, circuits
  - Sensors (8800-8999): Calibration, rationality
  - Predictive Maintenance (9000-9499): Wear analysis, trends
  - Load Management (9500-9699): Profile, factor, acceptance
  - Programming (9700-9999): Parameters, config, protocol

Total fault code coverage now exceeds 450,000+ across all subsystems.

commit.message:
Switch to music-trained CLAP model for instrument classification (#266)

The general-purpose CLAP model (clap-htsat-unfused) was biased toward
classifying every stem as vocals. Swap to the music-trained variant
(larger_clap_music_and_speech) which is specifically trained on music
and speech, improving classification accuracy for isolated instrument
stems.

Fixes #249

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WkVebxsmFyoPaPrtfkcG4M

commit.message:
fix(shiplocal): Redactor now detects emails/phones in kerned PDFs

LaTeX-generated PDFs split text into individual character items, so
"jerry@gmail.com" becomes ["jerry", "@", "gmail.com"]. The space-joined
text ("jerry @ gmail.com") broke the email regex.

Fix: run patterns against both space-joined and collapsed text. For
redaction drawing, detect multi-item spans that form a match and draw
a single rectangle covering the full span.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
